message:
fix(core): reject FTS5 alias operands, improve aux function errors, propagate group-by eval errors

Four interrelated fixes to match real SQLite FTS5 behavior and improve
error reporting in aggregate evaluation:

1. FTS5 alias rejection to match real SQLite:
   - Real SQLite does not allow table aliases in FTS5 MATCH operands or
     aux function arguments (e.g., "d MATCH 'query'" when "docs AS d")
   - Remove alias/col_map lookup fallback from is_vtab_table_match_operand,
     is_fts5_table_label_match, resolve_fts5_row_table_label, and
     try_eval_fts5_aux_function
   - Simplify collect_fts5_match_queries_for_source and
     extract_live_vtab_constraint_candidate by dropping alias parameter
   - Add three tests verifying alias rejection matches SQLite behavior

2. FTS5 aux function error reporting:
   - Add is_fts5_aux_function_name() helper
   - eval_join_expr and eval_group_agg_join_expr now return explicit
     "unable to use function {name} in the requested context" error when
     an FTS5 aux function (bm25, highlight, snippet) is called but
     try_eval_fts5_aux_function returns None, instead of falling through
     to generic scalar function evaluation

3. FTS5 aux context during primary scan filtering:
   - maybe_filter_primary_join_rows gains fts5_aux_context parameter
   - When present, pushes the FTS5 aux context guard before evaluating
     the predicate on each row
   - All call sites updated to pass context when non-empty

4. Error propagation in GROUP BY / HAVING / SELECT evaluation:
   - Change eval_join_expr_with_using return type from SqliteValue to
     Result<SqliteValue>
   - Four code sections in group-by paths changed from swallowing errors
     with .unwrap_or(SqliteValue::Null) to propagating with ?
   - Evaluation errors (e.g., FTS5 aux in wrong context) now surface
     to the caller instead of silently returning NULL

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
